title: user message
summary: **Constraints:** - Use less than 5 sub-structures, each sub-structure must be less than 100 blocks. - The bridge must be designed in the style of London Tower Bridge. - The structure must include at least two bridge tow…
body:
**Constraints:**
- Use less than 5 sub-structures, each sub-structure must be less than 100 blocks.
- The bridge must be designed in the style of London Tower Bridge.
- The structure must include at least two bridge towers.
- The bridge deck must be suspended using rope elements.
- Temporary scaffolds may be used during construction and then removed afterward to create hollow sections, layered forms, or height variation.

**Goal:**
- Build a highly elaborate and visually striking bridge inspired by London Tower Bridge using only one sub-structure.
- The design should emphasize architectural beauty, symmetry, vertical layering, and rich ornamental detail.
- The bridge must span a 10-unit gap between two flat terrains.
- The bridge deck must be suspended by ropes connected to the towers.
- The bridge deck must be at least 5 units above the bottom of the gap.
- The design should make extensive use of diverse block types to maximize artistic expression and construction complexity.

**Evaluation Protocol:**
- The terrains are positioned to form a 10-unit-wide gap along the north–south axis.
- The bridge will be initially placed above the gap so it can gently fall into position.
- There will be no fixed connection between the bridge and the terrain.
- The bridge must successfully span the full 10-unit gap using only one sub-structure.
- The final structure must include at least two distinct towers rising above the deck.
- The bridge deck must be suspended from the towers using rope elements rather than supported only from below.
- Temporary scaffold-based construction is allowed during assembly, and scaffold elements may be removed afterward to achieve hollow interiors, open frames, suspended details, or multiple height levels.
- The bridge deck must remain at a height of at least 5 units above the bottom of the gap.
- The structure will be evaluated for successful spanning, compliance with the Tower Bridge-inspired form, suspended deck design, visual richness, and overall architectural complexity.
- Designs that fail to span the gap, lack the required towers, fail to suspend the deck with ropes, or do not satisfy the deck-height requirement will receive a score of 0.

**Scoring Metrics:**
- *Structural Validity:* Whether the bridge successfully spans the 10-unit gap with a deck height of at least 5 units.
- *Tower Bridge Style Fidelity:* How strongly the final form resembles a London Tower Bridge-like design, including multiple towers and suspended deck composition.
- *Suspension Design Quality:* Effectiveness and elegance of the rope-supported bridge deck.
- *Aesthetic Complexity:* Degree of ornamentation, layered detail, hollow construction, and architectural sophistication.
- *Block Variety:* Number and diversity of block types used in the design (more is better).
- *Cost:* Number of blocks used to build the bridge.

title: planner message
summary: <building_plan> <overall_structure> <description> A single, self-contained bridge sub-structure that evokes London’s Tower Bridge: two prominent portal-style towers flanking the roadway, an elevated deck suspended from…
body:
<building_plan>
  <overall_structure>
    <description>
      A single, self-contained bridge sub-structure that evokes London’s Tower Bridge: two prominent portal-style towers flanking the roadway, an elevated deck suspended from the towers via multiple rope winches, and rich layered detailing with open frames and lantern-like torches. The composition is symmetric about the east–west axis (x) and spans along the north–south axis (y) to cross the 10‑unit gap. Each tower is a paired-column frame (two vertical legs per tower) with open interior voids and a high “saddle” level to attach suspension ropes. The deck runs between the towers at z≈5 and is intentionally separated from the tower legs at deck level to create a true suspended deck, leaving tall portal openings and hollow voids to emphasize vertical layering and ornamental complexity.
    </description>
    <functionality>
      The bridge spans a 10‑unit north–south gap with its deck at least 5 units above the bottom of the gap (deck plane set at z≈5). The two towers rest on the banks via their lower legs (down to z≈0), while winch ropes (acting as hanging suspenders) connect the tower tops to multiple points along the deck to carry its vertical load. Braces are used as rigid, volumeless cross-ties and diagonals to lock the frame and keep all elements unified as one sub-structure after temporary scaffold removal. Powered wheels serve as decorative “clock faces/gears,” torches as tower lanterns (flame kept safely outside), and a single water cannon as a decorative “nozzle” finial (controls unbound).
    </functionality>
    <assembly>
      The entire bridge is constructed as one sub-structure starting from the default 1×1×1 base stone block. First, a tall central kingpost raises the build plane to z≈5. A straight deck spine is extended along the y-axis to a total length of about 12 units (overhanging the 10‑unit gap). At y≈±5, short lateral arms reach outward to establish the tower footprints at x=±2. Each tower’s two vertical legs are then built up (to z≈8–9) and down (to z≈0), and light-weight braces rigidly couple the legs at high and low levels. Winches are hung from each tower’s high level to multiple deck points to suspend the deck. With ropes in place, temporary lateral arm-blocks at deck level are removed to open the portal voids so the deck hangs primarily from the winches. Finally, decorative powered wheels, torches, and one water cannon are added for stylistic richness.
    </assembly>
    <motion_control>
      - Powered Wheels: Leave controls unbound or do not press their keys so they remain braked (used as static rotating “clock-face” ornaments only if desired during showcase).
      - Water Cannon: Leave control unbound; do not fire (avoid any recoil).
      - Winches (rope elements): Keep at neutral length once the deck is suspended. No dynamic adjustment required; they act as static hangers.
      - The structure is otherwise static. It will be placed above the gap and gently fall until the tower lower legs make contact with the banks, seating the span. No connection to terrain is needed.
    </motion_control>
  </overall_structure>

  <sub_structures>
    <sub_structure_1>
      <name>Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span</name>
      <description>
        A one-piece bridge incorporating: two open-frame portal towers at y≈±5 with legs at x=±2; a suspended deck along y from about −6 to +6 at z≈5; multiple rope winch hangers from tower tops to deck; and decorative elements (powered wheels as “clock faces,” torches as lanterns, and a water cannon finial). Temporary deck-level arms used for tower erection are removed after rope suspension to create the characteristic hollowed arches and vertical layering.
      </description>
      <components>
        - Base: 1 default 1×1×1 stone starting block (mandatory).
        - Small Wooden Block (approx. 78–80 final count, kept < 100 total including connectors):
          - Central kingpost and deck spine along y (≈12).
          - Tower lateral arm stubs to x=±2 at y=±5 for each side, used in erection then partially removed.
          - Four tower legs (two per tower): each leg extends up ≈3 blocks above deck and down ≈5 blocks below deck.
          - Minimal ornamental caps and small ledges at tower tops.
        - Winch (8 total):
          - 4 on the north tower, 4 on the south tower (acting as rope suspenders to the deck).
        - Brace (6 total):
          - 2 top cross-ties (one per tower, linking the two legs at high level).
          - 2 low cross-ties (one per tower, linking the two legs near the base).
          - 2 diagonal ties from deck to lower tower legs (one per tower) to preserve lateral unity after scaffold removal.
        - Powered Wheel (2 total):
          - Decorative “clock-face” discs, one centered on each tower’s outer face.
        - Torch (4 total):
          - Lanterns on tower exteriors, oriented so the 0.3-radius flame sphere is outside wood surfaces.
        - Water Cannon (1 total):
          - Decorative finial mounted high, control unbound, not fired.
      </components>
      <assembly>
        1) Establish the deck elevation and spine
           - From the default base stone block, stack 5 Small Wooden Blocks vertically to create a central kingpost (sets the working deck elevation at z≈5).
           - From the kingpost’s top, extend a straight Small Wooden Block chain along the y-axis to form a deck spine ≈12 blocks long (from about y=−6 to y=+6 at x=0, z≈5). This assures a span longer than the 10‑unit gap.

        2) Create tower arm stubs at y≈±5
           - At the deck blocks located at y=+5 and y=−5 (x=0, z≈5), attach two-block lateral arms in both +x and −x directions:
             • At each y=±5 position, place a block on the +x face (x=+1), then another on the +x face of that block (x=+2).
             • Repeat to the −x side (x=−1 and x=−2).
           - These 4 arms (two at each tower location) temporarily couple the upcoming tower legs to the deck for erection.

        3) Build the tower legs (two towers, each with two legs at x=±2, y=±5)
           - For each of the four arm tips (x=+2 and x=−2 at y=+5, and x=+2 and x=−2 at y=−5), create vertical leg columns:
             • Upward: stack 3 Small Wooden Blocks (to about z≈8).
             • Downward: stack 5 Small Wooden Blocks (to about z≈0) so the feet will contact the banks when the structure settles.
           - This forms two portal frames: a north tower at y=+5 and a south tower at y=−5.

        4) Cross-tie the tower legs with volumeless Braces
           - High-level cap: add 1 Brace per tower between the inner faces of the two top leg blocks (z≈8–9), creating a rigid top tie that also serves as a rope anchor “saddle.”
           - Low-level tie: add 1 Brace per tower between the inner faces of the two lower leg blocks near z≈1–2 (below deck), stabilizing the base and reinforcing the open lower arch look.

        5) Suspend the deck with Winches (ropes)
           - North tower (y=+5):
             • From the −y faces of both top leg blocks (x=−2 and x=+2, z≈8), run 4 Winches down to the top faces of deck blocks at y=+4 and y=+2 (two anchors per y).
           - South tower (y=−5):
             • From the +y faces of both top leg blocks (x=−2 and x=+2, z≈8), run 4 Winches down to the top faces of deck blocks at y=−4 and y=−2 (two anchors per y).
           - This creates a set of symmetrical rope hangers between towers and deck, allowing a gentle “sagged” suspended appearance and ensuring the deck’s vertical support is via ropes.

        6) Preserve overall unity while freeing the deck (scaffold removal and lateral ties)
           - Before removal: add 1 Brace per tower diagonally from the deck block at y=±5 (use a side face) to a lower leg block at the same tower (e.g., to the inner face of the leg near z≈2). These braces provide lateral linkage so the whole build remains a single sub-structure after removing arm blocks.
           - Remove the inner deck-level scaffold blocks at x=±1 at y=±5 (the intermediate arm blocks that directly touch the deck). Leave the outermost arm tip blocks at x=±2 only if still needed to access/retain tower alignment during build; ideally remove those as well if the diagonal brace is in place and the tower is fully stable. The goal is to leave the deck visually and structurally suspended by the winch ropes while keeping a minimal lateral brace link to satisfy single-piece assembly and provide horizontal stability.

        7) Add ornamental elements
           - Powered Wheels (2): Attach one to each tower’s outer vertical face at about z≈7 (east face on the east leg and west face on the west leg), acting as rotating “clock gears.” Do not press their control keys so they remain stopped/braked unless a visual spin is desired during display.
           - Torches (4): Mount as lanterns on the outer faces of the legs (e.g., at z≈6–7), oriented outward so the flame’s 0.3‑unit heating sphere is fully outside the wood structure. Avoid placing near Winch ropes. Example: attach a torch to the east face of the east leg and point it further east; attach a torch to the west face of the west leg and point it further west on both towers.
           - Water Cannon (1): Place centrally above the deck around y≈0, x≈0, z≈7 as a decorative finial pointing upward or outward. Leave controls unbound so it never fires (prevents recoil and inadvertent torch extinguishing).
      </assembly>
      <motion_control>
        - Do not bind or press Water Cannon controls; it must remain idle.
        - Do not bind or press Powered Wheel controls unless a static visual spin is desired briefly; otherwise keep them braked.
        - Winches: Once connected, leave at default length so the deck hangs under gravity. No active control needed during evaluation.
        - The structure is static; it will be placed above the gap and allowed to settle with the tower feet contacting the banks. The deck remains ≥5 units above the gap bottom by design (deck elevation set at z≈5 relative to tower foot pads).
      </motion_control>
      <function>
        - The two portal towers supply vertical elevation and rope attachment points; their lower legs act as feet to rest on the banks.
        - The deck spans the 10‑unit north–south gap with about 12 units overall length to ensure overlap and seating.
        - Winches (ropes) provide primary vertical support, suspending the deck from the tower tops in the spirit of London Tower Bridge.
        - Braces lock the towers internally and provide minimal lateral ties to the deck after scaffold removal, preserving a single-piece assembly and resisting sway.
        - Decorative wheels, torches, and the water cannon enhance architectural richness and block diversity without altering stability.
      </function>
      <design_requirements>
        - Orientation: The bridge spans along the north–south (y) axis; place so towers align near y≈±5.
        - Dimensions: Deck elevation ≈ z=5; tower tops ≈ z=8–9; tower feet down to ≈ z=0. Deck length ≈ 12 units (y from −6 to +6), ensuring full coverage of the 10‑unit gap.
        - Suspension: Ensure all 8 winches are attached from tower-top inner faces to deck top faces at y=±4 and ±2. After attaching, remove the intermediate arm blocks at x=±1 at y=±5 to leave the deck primarily suspended by ropes.
        - Bracing: Keep 6 Braces total—two top cross-ties (one per tower), two low cross-ties (one per tower), and two diagonal deck-to-tower ties (one per tower) to maintain single-piece integrity and lateral stability.
        - Fire safety: Orient torches outward so the 0.3‑unit flame sphere does not intersect any wood, rope, or cannon body. Do not fire the Water Cannon.
        - Controls: Leave Water Cannon and Wheels unbound/inactive during evaluation.
        - Block count: Keep total under 100 for this single sub-structure; target ≈99 or fewer after scaffold removal (≈78–80 Small Wooden Blocks, 8 Winches, 6 Braces, 2 Powered Wheels, 4 Torches, 1 Water Cannon, plus the default base stone).
        - Positions may be micro‑adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
      </design_requirements>
    </sub_structure_1>
  </sub_structures>
</building_plan>

title: drafter message
summary: Sub-structure: Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span Global axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. A default 1×1×1 base block exists at [0,0,0]; all first attachments reference its faces as needed. All blocks are attached to existing…
body:
Sub-structure: Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span
Global axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. A default 1×1×1 base block exists at [0,0,0]; all first attachments reference its faces as needed. All blocks are attached to existing block faces; connectors link faces of two existing blocks. Final target span runs along the y-axis from ≈y=−6 to y=+6 with deck at z≈5, towers at y≈±5, legs at x=±2.

Block declarations (in build order; each line shows attachment and, when not on the immediately previous block, the specific anchor block face is identified)

Deck elevation and spine (kingpost up to z≈5, then spine y=−6..+6)
1 - Small Wooden Block - Kingpost segment z=1 above base - top of base
2 - Small Wooden Block - Kingpost segment z=2 - top of previous
3 - Small Wooden Block - Kingpost segment z=3 - top of previous
4 - Small Wooden Block - Kingpost segment z=4 - top of previous
5 - Small Wooden Block - Kingpost head (deck node y=0, z=5) - top of previous
6 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+1 (z≈5) - north of Block 5 (attached to Block 5 north face)
7 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+2 - north of previous
8 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+3 - north of previous
9 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+4 - north of previous
10 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+5 (north tower deck node) - north of previous
11 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=+6 (north approach) - north of previous
12 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−1 - south of Block 5 (attached to Block 5 south face)
13 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−2 - south of previous
14 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−3 - south of previous
15 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−4 - south of previous
16 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−5 (south tower deck node) - south of previous
17 - Small Wooden Block - Deck spine y=−6 (south approach) - south of previous

Deck side wideners at selected y to improve rope load distribution (12 blocks total; all at z≈5)
18 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=+4 - east of Block 9 (attached to Block 9 east face)
19 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=+4 - west of Block 9 (attached to Block 9 west face)
20 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=+2 - east of Block 7 (attached to Block 7 east face)
21 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=+2 - west of Block 7 (attached to Block 7 west face)
22 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=−2 - east of Block 13 (attached to Block 13 east face)
23 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=−2 - west of Block 13 (attached to Block 13 west face)
24 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=−4 - east of Block 15 (attached to Block 15 east face)
25 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=−4 - west of Block 15 (attached to Block 15 west face)
26 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=+6 - east of Block 11 (attached to Block 11 east face)
27 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=+6 - west of Block 11 (attached to Block 11 west face)
28 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener east at y=−6 - east of Block 17 (attached to Block 17 east face)
29 - Small Wooden Block - Deck widener west at y=−6 - west of Block 17 (attached to Block 17 west face)

North tower (y=+5): east leg at x=+2, west leg at x=−2; inner deck-level arm blocks at x=±1 are temporary scaffold to be removed in step 6
30 - Small Wooden Block - TEMP scaffold arm from deck y=+5 to x=+1 - east of Block 10 (to be removed after winches/diagonal brace)
31 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg deck node (x=+2, z=5) - east of previous
32 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=6 - top of previous
33 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=7 - top of previous
34 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=8 (top leg block) - top of previous
35 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=4 - bottom of Block 31
36 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=3 - bottom of previous
37 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=2 - bottom of previous
38 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg z=1 - bottom of previous
39 - Small Wooden Block - North tower east leg foot z=0 - bottom of previous
40 - Small Wooden Block - TEMP scaffold arm from deck y=+5 to x=−1 - west of Block 10 (to be removed after winches/diagonal brace)
41 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg deck node (x=−2, z=5) - west of previous
42 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=6 - top of previous
43 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=7 - top of previous
44 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=8 (top leg block) - top of previous
45 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=4 - bottom of Block 41
46 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=3 - bottom of previous
47 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=2 - bottom of previous
48 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg z=1 - bottom of previous
49 - Small Wooden Block - North tower west leg foot z=0 - bottom of previous

South tower (y=−5): east leg at x=+2, west leg at x=−2; inner deck-level arm blocks at x=±1 are temporary scaffold to be removed in step 6
50 - Small Wooden Block - TEMP scaffold arm from deck y=−5 to x=+1 - east of Block 16 (to be removed after winches/diagonal brace)
51 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east leg deck node (x=+2, z=5) - east of previous
52 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east leg z=6 - top of previous
53 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east leg z=7 - top of previous

56 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east leg z=3 - bottom of previous

59 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east leg foot z=0 - bottom of previous

Tower ornamental caps and ledges (kept small to preserve open-frame portals)

76 - Small Wooden Block - South tower east outward ledge (from cap) - east of Block 72
77 - Small Wooden Block - South tower west outward ledge (from cap) - west of Block 73

Finial pedestal for water cannon

Powered wheel ornaments (2 total; one per tower outer face)
79 - Powered Wheel - North tower “clock-face” on east leg, centered at z≈7 - east of Block 33 - Function: Decorative powered wheel mounted on a vertical east face; axis along +x (into leg), wheel plane vertical; if activated it would roll north–south parallel to the face. Keep control unbound/inactive so it remains braked.
80 - Powered Wheel - South tower “clock-face” on west leg, centered at z≈7 - west of Block 63 - Function: Decorative powered wheel mounted on a vertical west face; axis along −x, wheel plane vertical; if activated it would roll north–south. Keep control unbound/inactive so it remains braked.

Torches as lanterns (4 total; all oriented outward/up so flame spheres clear wood/rope)
81 - Torch - Lantern on north tower east leg at z≈6, flame outward/up - east of Block 32 - Function: Oriented upward; heating sphere radius 0.3 centered ≈1 unit outward and 1 unit above the leg face (clear of wood and ropes). Does not contact wheel (≥0.2 unit clearance in x).
82 - Torch - Lantern on north tower west leg at z≈6, flame outward/up - west of Block 42 - Function: Oriented upward; flame sphere fully outside wood and away from ropes/wheel.
83 - Torch - Lantern on south tower east leg at z≈6, flame outward/up - east of Block 52 - Function: Oriented upward; flame sphere fully outside wood and away from ropes.
84 - Torch - Lantern on south tower west leg at z≈6, flame outward/up - west of Block 62 - Function: Oriented upward; flame sphere fully outside wood and away from ropes.

Water cannon finial (leave idle)
85 - Water Cannon - Decorative finial centered above deck (y≈0), pointing up - top of Block 78 - Function: Axis vertical; recoil intentionally unused. Leave controls unbound; do not fire. Inlet near z≈5.625, outlet near z≈7.375; body well clear of torches’ steam/fire and ropes.

Structural braces (6 total; volumeless connectors linking two faces)
86 - Brace - North tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 34 west face and Block 44 east face - Note: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two north tower legs internally.
87 - Brace - South tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 54 west face and Block 64 east face - Note: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two south tower legs.
88 - Brace - North tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 37 west face and Block 47 east face - Note: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
89 - Brace - South tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 57 west face and Block 67 east face - Note: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
90 - Brace - North diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 10 east face and Block 37 west face - Note: Install after removing temp arm Block 30; preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability.
91 - Brace - South diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 16 west face and Block 67 east face - Note: Install after removing temp arm Block 60; preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability.

Suspension winches (8 total; rope hangers from tower tops to deck top faces)
92 - Winch - North tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-east top.
93 - Winch - North tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-west top.
94 - Winch - North tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-east top.
95 - Winch - North tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-west top.
96 - Winch - South tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-east top.
97 - Winch - South tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-west top.
98 - Winch - South tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-east top.
99 - Winch - South tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-west top.

Scaffold removal (after all winches 92–99 and diagonal braces 90–91 are installed)
- Remove Block 30 (temp arm x=+1 at y=+5), Block 40 (temp arm x=−1 at y=+5), Block 50 (temp arm x=+1 at y=−5), and Block 60 (temp arm x=−1 at y=−5). This frees the deck under each tower while maintaining a single-piece assembly via ropes and diagonal braces.

Functional behavior and controls
- Powered Wheels (Blocks 79, 80): Decorative only; leave controls unbound/off so they remain braked. If enabled, each vertical disc would produce rolling along the north–south axis parallel to its attached face.
- Torches (Blocks 81–84): Oriented upward/outward; each flame sphere (radius 0.3) is centered 1 unit outward and 1 unit above its mounting point, clear of wood/rope/wheel surfaces. Avoids accidental ignition.
- Water Cannon (Block 85): Decorative finial, pointing upward. Leave control unbound; do not fire. No recoil will be produced.
- Winches (Blocks 92–99): Leave at default length for a gentle catenary; no active control required.

Position adjustment notes
- Raised finial: Added small wooden pedestal (Block 78) so the Water Cannon center sits ≈z=6.5 and outlet ≈z=7.375, matching the “around z≈7” design intent.
- Wheel clearance: Outward ledges were attached to cap blocks at z=9 (Blocks 74–77) rather than to z=8 leg faces to ensure the 2‑unit-diameter wheel discs at z≈7 have full clearance (no coplanar overlap).
- Torch clearance: Torches placed one block below wheels (z≈6). With flame sphere center ~1 unit outward and upward from the mounting face and sphere radius 0.3, minimum clearance to the wheel body is ≥0.2 units in x, preventing heating/ignition of the wheels.

Final block count (after scaffold removal)
- Small Wooden Blocks: 78
- Braces: 6
- Winches: 8
- Powered Wheels: 2
- Torches: 4
- Water Cannon: 1
Total: 99 (under 100), plus the default base.

Assembly rationale
- Deck spine spans y≈−6..+6 at z≈5 to clear a 10‑unit gap.
- Two portal towers at y=±5 with legs at x=±2 rise to z≈8–9 and drop to z≈0, with internal braces for rigidity.
- Eight winch hangers suspend the deck from tower tops (y=±5 → deck y=±4, ±2).
- After installing winches and diagonal braces, temporary deck-level arm blocks at x=±1 are removed to reveal the hollowed arches while retaining lateral ties for single-piece integrity.

title: draft_reviewer message
summary: Reviewer analysis for “Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span” Conventions - Coordinate frame: +x east, +y north, +z up. Base block (1×1×1) center at [0,0,0]. Small Wooden Blocks (SWB) are 1×1×1 cubes. - When a SWB is attached…
body:
Reviewer analysis for “Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span”

Conventions
- Coordinate frame: +x east, +y north, +z up. Base block (1×1×1) center at [0,0,0]. Small Wooden Blocks (SWB) are 1×1×1 cubes.
- When a SWB is attached to a face of another SWB, centers differ by exactly 1 along that face-normal axis. Minimum non-overlap separation along that axis is 1 (0.5 + 0.5), so face-to-face touching at 1 unit is acceptable.
- Powered Wheel shape: [2,2,0.5]; axis perpendicular to attached face; we assume the wheel center lies 0.25 outward from the face (thickness/2) unless otherwise specified. Disc radius = 1 in the wheel plane. We will flag where this assumption matters.
- Torch: using the given example, torch body center is 0.5 outboard from the mounting face along the face normal. If the torch is oriented “up,” the heating sphere center is 1 unit above the torch body center (i.e., body + [0,0,1]) and has radius 0.3.
- Water Cannon: center is 0.5 away from the mounting face along the orientation direction (as per the official example). Length along axis 1.75; endpoints are ±0.875 from center along the axis.

Step-by-step structural placement and collision checks
Note: For each block, we report its center coordinates and check potential-overlap neighbors (typically the block it attaches to and any blocks whose AABBs could touch). Face-adjacent SWBs have center-to-center distance = 1 along the attach axis and 0 along the other two axes; this is the minimum allowed, i.e., “just touching faces.” We flag any deviation.

Base
- Base (implicit): [0,0,0].

Deck elevation and spine
1. SWB [0,0,1] (top of base). Neighbor: Base [0,0,0], Δz=1 (OK).
2. SWB [0,0,2] (top of 1). Neighbor: 1, Δz=1 (OK).
3. SWB [0,0,3] (top of 2). Neighbor: 2, Δz=1 (OK).
4. SWB [0,0,4] (top of 3). Neighbor: 3, Δz=1 (OK).
5. SWB [0,0,5] (top of 4). Neighbor: 4, Δz=1 (OK).
6. SWB [0,1,5] (north of 5). Neighbor: 5, Δy=1 (OK).
7. SWB [0,2,5] (north of 6). Neighbor: 6, Δy=1 (OK).
8. SWB [0,3,5] (north of 7). Neighbor: 7, Δy=1 (OK).
9. SWB [0,4,5] (north of 8). Neighbor: 8, Δy=1 (OK).
10. SWB [0,5,5] (north of 9). Neighbor: 9, Δy=1 (OK).
11. SWB [0,6,5] (north of 10). Neighbor: 10, Δy=1 (OK).
12. SWB [0,−1,5] (south of 5). Neighbor: 5, Δy=1 (OK).
13. SWB [0,−2,5] (south of 12). Neighbor: 12, Δy=1 (OK).
14. SWB [0,−3,5] (south of 13). Neighbor: 13, Δy=1 (OK).
15. SWB [0,−4,5] (south of 14). Neighbor: 14, Δy=1 (OK).
16. SWB [0,−5,5] (south of 15). Neighbor: 15, Δy=1 (OK).
17. SWB [0,−6,5] (south of 16). Neighbor: 16, Δy=1 (OK).

Deck side wideners (all at z=5)
18. SWB [1,4,5] (east of 9). Neighbors: 9, Δx=1 (OK). Check vs 19: center separation 2 along x (OK).
19. SWB [−1,4,5] (west of 9). Neighbor: 9, Δx=1 (OK).
20. SWB [1,2,5] (east of 7). Neighbor: 7, Δx=1 (OK). vs 21: separation 2 along x (OK).
21. SWB [−1,2,5] (west of 7). Neighbor: 7, Δx=1 (OK).
22. SWB [1,−2,5] (east of 13). Neighbor: 13, Δx=1 (OK). vs 23: sep 2 (OK).
23. SWB [−1,−2,5] (west of 13). Neighbor: 13, Δx=1 (OK).
24. SWB [1,−4,5] (east of 15). Neighbor: 15, Δx=1 (OK). vs 25: sep 2 (OK).
25. SWB [−1,−4,5] (west of 15). Neighbor: 15, Δx=1 (OK).
26. SWB [1,6,5] (east of 11). Neighbor: 11, Δx=1 (OK). vs 27: sep 2 (OK).
27. SWB [−1,6,5] (west of 11). Neighbor: 11, Δx=1 (OK).
28. SWB [1,−6,5] (east of 17). Neighbor: 17, Δx=1 (OK). vs 29: sep 2 (OK).
29. SWB [−1,−6,5] (west of 17). Neighbor: 17, Δx=1 (OK).

North tower (y=+5). Note: 30 and 40 are temporary scaffold to be removed later.
30. SWB [1,5,5] (east of 10). Neighbor: 10, Δx=1 (OK).
31. SWB [2,5,5] (east of 30). Neighbors: 30 Δx=1 (OK); vs 10 Δx=2 (OK).
32. SWB [2,5,6] (top of 31). Neighbor: 31 Δz=1 (OK).
33. SWB [2,5,7] (top of 32). Neighbor: 32 Δz=1 (OK).
34. SWB [2,5,8] (top of 33). Neighbor: 33 Δz=1 (OK).
35. SWB [2,5,4] (bottom of 31). Neighbor: 31 Δz=1 (OK).
36. SWB [2,5,3] (bottom of 35). Neighbor: 35 Δz=1 (OK).
37. SWB [2,5,2] (bottom of 36). Neighbor: 36 Δz=1 (OK).
38. SWB [2,5,1] (bottom of 37). Neighbor: 37 Δz=1 (OK).
39. SWB [2,5,0] (bottom of 38). Neighbor: 38 Δz=1 (OK).
40. SWB [−1,5,5] (west of 10). Neighbor: 10 Δx=1 (OK).
41. SWB [−2,5,5] (west of 40). Neighbors: 40 Δx=1 (OK); vs 10 Δx=2 (OK).
42. SWB [−2,5,6] (top of 41). Neighbor: 41 Δz=1 (OK).
43. SWB [−2,5,7] (top of 42). Neighbor: 42 Δz=1 (OK).
44. SWB [−2,5,8] (top of 43). Neighbor: 43 Δz=1 (OK).
45. SWB [−2,5,4] (bottom of 41). Neighbor: 41 Δz=1 (OK).
46. SWB [−2,5,3] (bottom of 45). Neighbor: 45 Δz=1 (OK).
47. SWB [−2,5,2] (bottom of 46). Neighbor: 46 Δz=1 (OK).
48. SWB [−2,5,1] (bottom of 47). Neighbor: 47 Δz=1 (OK).
49. SWB [−2,5,0] (bottom of 48). Neighbor: 48 Δz=1 (OK).

South tower (y=−5). 50 and 60 are temporary scaffold.
50. SWB [1,−5,5] (east of 16). Neighbor: 16 Δx=1 (OK).
51. SWB [2,−5,5] (east of 50). Neighbors: 50 Δx=1 (OK); vs 16 Δx=2 (OK).
52. SWB [2,−5,6] (top of 51). Neighbor: 51 Δz=1 (OK).
53. SWB [2,−5,7] (top of 52). Neighbor: 52 Δz=1 (OK).
54. SWB [2,−5,8] (top of 53). Neighbor: 53 Δz=1 (OK).
55. SWB [2,−5,4] (bottom of 51). Neighbor: 51 Δz=1 (OK).
56. SWB [2,−5,3] (bottom of 55). Neighbor: 55 Δz=1 (OK).
57. SWB [2,−5,2] (bottom of 56). Neighbor: 56 Δz=1 (OK).
58. SWB [2,−5,1] (bottom of 57). Neighbor: 57 Δz=1 (OK).
59. SWB [2,−5,0] (bottom of 58). Neighbor: 58 Δz=1 (OK).
60. SWB [−1,−5,5] (west of 16). Neighbor: 16 Δx=1 (OK).
61. SWB [−2,−5,5] (west of 60). Neighbors: 60 Δx=1 (OK); vs 16 Δx=2 (OK).
62. SWB [−2,−5,6] (top of 61). Neighbor: 61 Δz=1 (OK).
63. SWB [−2,−5,7] (top of 62). Neighbor: 62 Δz=1 (OK).
64. SWB [−2,−5,8] (top of 63). Neighbor: 63 Δz=1 (OK).
65. SWB [−2,−5,4] (bottom of 61). Neighbor: 61 Δz=1 (OK).
66. SWB [−2,−5,3] (bottom of 65). Neighbor: 65 Δz=1 (OK).
67. SWB [−2,−5,2] (bottom of 66). Neighbor: 66 Δz=1 (OK).
68. SWB [−2,−5,1] (bottom of 67). Neighbor: 67 Δz=1 (OK).
69. SWB [−2,−5,0] (bottom of 68). Neighbor: 68 Δz=1 (OK).

Tower caps and ledges
70. SWB [2,5,9] (top of 34). Neighbor: 34 Δz=1 (OK).
71. SWB [−2,5,9] (top of 44). Neighbor: 44 Δz=1 (OK).
72. SWB [2,−5,9] (top of 54). Neighbor: 54 Δz=1 (OK).
73. SWB [−2,−5,9] (top of 64). Neighbor: 64 Δz=1 (OK).
74. SWB [3,5,9] (east of 70). Neighbors: 70 Δx=1 (OK). Check against wheel at z≈7 later: clear in z.
75. SWB [−3,5,9] (west of 71). Neighbor: 71 Δx=1 (OK).
76. SWB [3,−5,9] (east of 72). Neighbor: 72 Δx=1 (OK).
77. SWB [−3,−5,9] (west of 73). Neighbor: 73 Δx=1 (OK).

Finial pedestal
78. SWB [0,0,6] (top of 5). Neighbor: 5 Δz=1 (OK).

Powered wheels (ornaments)
Assumption: wheel center at 0.25 outboard from mounting face; thickness spans from the face plane to +0.5 outboard; disc radius = 1 in (y,z).
79. Powered Wheel attached to east face of Block 33 ([2,5,7]):
- Face plane x=2.5. Wheel center ≈ [2.75,5,7]. Wheel spans in x ≈ [2.5,3.0], in y ≈ [4,6], in z ≈ [6,8].
- Neighbor distances: to Block 33 center [2,5,7] is Δx=0.75 (no collision; intended attachment) with coplanar inner face touching at x=2.5. Check against ledge 74 [3,5,9]: Δz=2, Δx=0.25, but z separation > radius, no overlap. Check Torch 81 below (see torch analysis).
80. Powered Wheel attached to west face of Block 63 ([−2,−5,7]):
- Face plane x=−2.5. Wheel center ≈ [−2.75,−5,7]. Wheel spans x≈[−3.0,−2.5], y≈[−6,−4], z≈[6,8].
- Neighbor distances: to Block 63 [−2,−5,7] Δx=0.75 (OK). Check ledge 77 [−3,−5,9], Δz=2, Δx=0.25—no overlap. Check Torch 84 below (see torch analysis).

Torches (lanterns)
Using the example rule: torch body center is 0.5 outboard from mount face; oriented “upward” means flame sphere center is body + [0,0,1].
81. Torch on east face of Block 32 ([2,5,6]):
- Torch body center ≈ [3.0,5,6]. Flame sphere center ≈ [3.0,5,7], r=0.3.
- Neighbor of concern: Wheel 79 spans x∈[2.5,3.0], y∈[4,6], z∈[6,8]. Sphere center lies exactly at the wheel’s outer x-face (x=3.0), same y=5 and z=7. The 0.3-radius sphere penetrates inward to x=2.7, which intersects the wheel’s volume. This violates the fire-safety requirement (sphere must be fully outside wood/wheel).
82. Torch on west face of Block 42 ([−2,5,6]):
- Torch body center ≈ [−3.0,5,6]. Flame sphere ≈ [−3.0,5,7], r=0.3.
- There is no wheel at y=+5 west face (north wheel is at east leg), but this sphere is still near wooden leg surfaces. The leg’s west face plane is x=−2.5; sphere center at x=−3.0 leaves only 0.5 clearance to the leg face; sphere radius 0.3 means the inner extent reaches x=−3.3 to −2.7; the portion at x=−2.7 is 0.2 away from the leg face x=−2.5—OK relative to wood if the sphere does not cross x=−2.5. However, because the sphere center is aligned in y,z with the ledge at [−3,5,9] only in y and x, z is separated by 2, so no risk. (Primary risk is wheel intersection, which isn’t present here.)
83. Torch on east face of Block 52 ([2,−5,6]):
- Torch body center ≈ [3.0,−5,6]. Flame sphere ≈ [3.0,−5,7], r=0.3.
- Neighbor of concern: No wheel on this east face at south tower (wheel is on west leg at y=−5). Clearance to leg is same as 81 but without a wheel directly in front. The sphere approaches the east face (x=2.5) to x=2.7; clearance 0.2 (barely OK to leg if the spec demands outside any wood; still not intersecting the leg face).
84. Torch on west face of Block 62 ([−2,−5,6]):
- Torch body center ≈ [−3.0,−5,6]. Flame sphere ≈ [−3.0,−5,7], r=0.3.
- Neighbor of concern: Wheel 80 spans x∈[−3.0,−2.5], y∈[−6,−4], z∈[6,8]. Sphere center [−3.0,−5,7] lies exactly on the wheel outer face x=−3.0 at matching y,z; sphere penetrates inward to x=−2.7, intersecting the wheel’s volume. Fire-safety violation identical to 81.

Water cannon finial
85. Water Cannon on top of Block 78 (pointing up):
- Mount face: Block 78 top face plane z=6.5. Per the official example, the cannon center should be 0.5 above the mount face along +z, so center should be [0,0,7.0]. Axial span: z ∈ [6.125, 7.875]. The plan text stated center ≈6.5 with outlet ≈7.375, which conflicts with the spec example. Please correct to center z=7.0. With corrected placement, clearances to torches and wheels are >5 units horizontally/vertically; no collision risks.

Braces (volumeless; existence/attachment checks)
86. Between 34 west face (x=1.5, y=5, z=8) and 44 east face (x=−1.5, y=5, z=8): both faces exist; valid.
87. Between 54 west face (x=1.5, y=−5, z=8) and 64 east face (x=−1.5, y=−5, z=8): valid.
88. Between 37 west face (x=1.5, y=5, z=2) and 47 east face (x=−1.5, y=5, z=2): valid.
89. Between 57 west face (x=1.5, y=−5, z=2) and 67 east face (x=−1.5, y=−5, z=2): valid.
90. Between 10 east face (x=0.5, y=5, z=5) and 37 west face (x=1.5, y=5, z=2): valid. (Installed after removing 30.)
91. Between 16 west face (x=−0.5, y=−5, z=5) and 67 east face (x=−1.5, y=−5, z=2): valid. (Installed after removing 60.)

Winches (volumeless; existence/attachment checks)
92. From 34 (−y face at y=4.5, x=2, z=8) to 9 (top face at z=5.5, x=0, y=4): valid.
93. From 44 (−y face at y=4.5, x=−2, z=8) to 9 (top face at z=5.5, x=0, y=4): valid.
94. From 34 (−y face) to 7 (top face at z=5.5, x=0, y=2): valid.
95. From 44 (−y face) to 7 (top face): valid.
96. From 54 (+y face at y=−4.5, x=2, z=8) to 15 (top face at z=5.5, x=0, y=−4): valid.
97. From 64 (+y face at y=−4.5, x=−2, z=8) to 15 (top face): valid.
98. From 54 (+y face) to 13 (top face at z=5.5, x=0, y=−2): valid.
99. From 64 (+y face) to 13 (top face): valid.

Scaffold removal
- Blocks 30, 40, 50, 60 to be removed after 90–91 and 92–99 are installed. Removal does not create gaps in connectivity due to diagonal braces and winches.

Functional validation
1) Motion allowances and constraints
- The structure is intended to be static. No moving linkages are proposed except powered wheels (if activated) and water cannon recoil (left inactive).
- Powered Wheels:
  • 79 on east face of 33 (vertical face). Axis perpendicular to face (≈+x), wheel plane vertical (y–z). If activated, rolling direction in-plane. Note: the component description contains an internal inconsistency: one bullet says “rolling direction is always parallel to the attached face,” while a later example claims “rolling direction is perpendicular to the side face.” Regardless, these wheels are decorative/inactive; axis/plane orientation are consistent with either interpretation.
  • 80 on west face of 63: analogous.
- Water Cannon 85:
  • Oriented upward; recoil unused. Must correct center height to z=7.0 per spec (see above). No attachable faces needed further, and none are used.
- Winches 92–99: Attachment points exist; geometry yields plausible rope hangers from tower tops to deck spine at y=±4 and ±2. They are volumeless, so no collision risk; they serve to “suspend” deck visually/structurally.

2) Alignment/mounting of moving components
- Wheels 79 and 80 are mounted to vertical faces with axes normal to those faces; OK.
- Water Cannon 85 mounting to Block 78 top face aligns axis with +z; OK once center is corrected.
- No powered wheels are on horizontal faces; no “bottom-face” mounting pitfalls.

3) Special safety and functional checks
- Torches 81 & 84: As currently specified (“oriented upward”), the heat sphere centers are [3.0,5,7] and [−3.0,−5,7] respectively and intersect the outer faces of Wheels 79 and 80. This violates “Fire safety: Orient torches outward so the 0.3-radius flame sphere is outside wood surfaces.” As written, this does not pass.
- Torches 82 & 83: Their heat spheres are close to wooden leg faces (0.2 m clearance at the inner extent). This narrowly clears the leg, but the spec requires the sphere “outside wood surfaces”; with 0.2 m clearance it is technically outside, but the margin is small. Still acceptable if maintained, but better to increase clearance.

Physical feasibility assessment
- Geometry: All SWBs are placed face-to-face with separation = 1 along attach axis; no overlaps detected among blocks 1–78.
- Powered wheels have no physical collision with wood blocks (assuming the 0.25 outboard center). They do, however, conflict with torch heat zones as noted.
- Water Cannon location must be adjusted to [0,0,7.0] (center) as per the given attachment rule; with that correction, it is well clear of wood and torches.
- Connectivity after scaffold removal remains single-piece via braces 90–91 and ropes 92–99.
- External stability on “banks” depends on environmental placement and is outside the blueprint; within this sub-structure, the geometry is consistent.

Block count check
- Small Wooden Blocks listed: 1–78 inclusive = 78 blocks BEFORE scaffold removal. You then remove 30, 40, 50, 60 (four blocks), so the final SWB count should be 74, not 78. Final total after removal should be:
  • SWB 74 + Braces 6 + Winches 8 + Powered Wheels 2 + Torches 4 + Water Cannon 1 = 95 blocks (plus the base), not 99. Still under the 100 limit. Please correct the tally.

Summary of pass/fail and required corrections
- Structural integrity and collision-free placement: PASS for all wooden blocks, braces, winches, and cannon body, assuming cannon center is corrected. FAIL for torch fire-safety due to heat-sphere intersection with powered wheels 79 and 80.
- Functional implementation: Mostly OK (decorative wheels inactive, cannon inactive), but torch orientation as stated (“upward”) creates an unsafe overlap with wheels.
- Physical feasibility: OK within the sub-structure scope; external seating on banks is out-of-scope.

Required changes to approve
1) Torch orientation/placement corrections to meet fire-safety:
   - Option A (recommended, minimal change): Keep torches mounted on east/west leg faces at z≈6, but orient the flame direction purely outward (±x) rather than upward. Then:
     • 81 (east face of 32): torch body ≈ [3.0,5,6]; flame sphere center ≈ [4.0,5,6], r=0.3 ⇒ clear of the wheel (nearest wheel surface at x≈3.0) by ≥0.7 m.
     • 84 (west face of 62): torch body ≈ [−3.0,−5,6]; flame sphere center ≈ [−4.0,−5,6], r=0.3 ⇒ clear of wheel surface at x≈−3.0 by ≥0.7 m.
     • 82 and 83: likewise orient purely outward to increase clearance from leg faces (sphere centers at x≈−4.0 and +4.0 respectively).
   - Option B: If upward aesthetic is required, relocate torches away from wheel planes (e.g., off the legs that carry wheels, or at different y such that the wheel’s (y,z) footprint doesn’t coincide) and ensure the heat sphere center is at least 0.3 m beyond any wood or wheel surface in all directions. Provide specific coordinates to validate.
2) Correct Water Cannon center height to z=7.0 (center at [0,0,7.0]) per the official attachment rule (0.5 offset from the mounting face).
3) Correct the final block count: 95 total (excluding the base), not 99.
4) Optional clarification: Specify the Powered Wheel attachment offset (e.g., confirm 0.25 m center offset from the mounting face). Our safety correction for torches (Option A) removes dependence on this, but clarity is beneficial.
5) Documentation: Update notes to reflect the corrected torch orientations and water cannon coordinates.

With the above corrections applied (especially torch orientation to avoid heating any wood or wheel), the design will meet structural, functional, and safety requirements.

Current status: FAIL (due to torch heat-sphere intersections with wheels 79 and 80, and water cannon coordinate mismatch and block tally error). Please revise as per the required changes and resubmit for approval.

Global axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. Default 1×1×1 base at [0,0,0]. All first attachments reference its faces. Corrections applied per review: torches reoriented outward to clear wheels; water cannon center clarified to z=7.0; final block count corrected; wheel mounting offset clarified.

Structural braces (6 total; volumeless connectors linking two faces)
86 - Brace - North tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 34 west face and Block 44 east face - Function: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two north tower legs.
87 - Brace - South tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 54 west face and Block 64 east face - Function: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two south tower legs.
88 - Brace - North tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 37 west face and Block 47 east face - Function: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
89 - Brace - South tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 57 west face and Block 67 east face - Function: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
90 - Brace - North diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 10 east face and Block 37 west face - Function: Install after removing temp arm Block 30 (face conflict) to preserve single-piece unity and lateral stability.
91 - Brace - South diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 16 west face and Block 67 east face - Function: Install after removing temp arm Block 60 (face conflict) to preserve single-piece unity and lateral stability.

Suspension winches (8 total; rope hangers from tower tops to deck top faces)
92 - Winch - North tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-east top.
93 - Winch - North tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-west top.
94 - Winch - North tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-east top.
95 - Winch - North tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-west top.
96 - Winch - South tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-east top.
97 - Winch - South tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-west top.
98 - Winch - South tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-east top.
99 - Winch - South tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-west top.

Scaffold removal (after winches 92–99 are installed and diagonal braces 90–91 are in place)
- Remove Block 30 (temp arm at y=+5, x=+1), Block 40 (temp arm at y=+5, x=−1), Block 50 (temp arm at y=−5, x=+1), and Block 60 (temp arm at y=−5, x=−1). This frees the deck under each tower while maintaining a single-piece assembly via ropes and diagonal braces.

Functional behavior and controls
- Powered Wheels (79, 80): Decorative; leave controls unbound/off so they remain braked. Mounted on vertical faces; planes vertical. If briefly activated, they would spin without intended locomotive function.
- Torches (81–84): All flames oriented purely outward (±x). Each heating sphere (radius 0.3) is fully outside all wood, ropes, and powered wheels by ≥0.7 m.
- Water Cannon (85): Decorative finial, axis +z. Center at z=7.0 per attachment rule. Leave control unbound; do not fire. No recoil used.
- Winches (92–99): Leave at default length for gentle sag; no active control required.

Position adjustment notes
- Torch orientation update: Changed all lanterns to purely outward (±x) to eliminate any possibility of heat-sphere intersections with wheels or wood. Sphere centers now at x≈±4.0, providing ≥0.7 m clearance to nearest surfaces.
- Water cannon center correction: Confirmed center is 0.5 above Block 78 top face (z=6.5), i.e., z=7.0, to match the official example.
- Wheel mounting clarification: Assumed wheel center is 0.25 m outboard from the mounting face (thickness 0.5), disc radius 1. This keeps the wheel body entirely clear of adjacent wood ledges.

Final block count (after scaffold removal)
- Small Wooden Blocks: 74 (Blocks 1–78, minus removed 30, 40, 50, 60)
- Braces: 6
- Winches: 8
- Powered Wheels: 2
- Torches: 4
- Water Cannon: 1
Total: 95 (plus the default base), meeting the <100 requirement.

Assembly rationale
- Deck spine spans y≈−6..+6 at z≈5; towers at y=±5 with legs at x=±2 rise to z≈8–9 and drop to z≈0.
- Eight winch hangers suspend the deck from tower tops at y=±5 to deck nodes y=±4, ±2.
- Temporary arms at x=±1 enable leg erection; after winches and diagonal braces are installed, arms are removed to reveal the open arches while preserving single-piece integrity and lateral stability.

title: draft_reviewer message
summary: Reviewer analysis for “Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span” (revised) Conventions and geometry assumptions - Axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. Default base block center at [0,0,0]. - Small Wooden Block (SWB) size 1×1×1; face-t…
body:
Reviewer analysis for “Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span” (revised)

Conventions and geometry assumptions
- Axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. Default base block center at [0,0,0].
- Small Wooden Block (SWB) size 1×1×1; face-to-face attachment offsets the center by exactly 1 unit along the face normal. Minimum non-overlap separation along any attach axis is 1 (0.5 + 0.5); face adjacency at distance 1 is OK.
- Powered Wheel shape: [2,2,0.5]. When attached to a vertical face, axis is normal to that face. We take the wheel center 0.25 m outboard of the face (thickness/2), so the x-span from the face is [0, 0.5] and the wheel radius in the face plane (y–z) is 1.
- Torch: Body center is 0.5 outboard from the mounting face along face normal. The flame sphere center is “body center + orientation vector” (length 1) in the given flame direction. Sphere radius r=0.3.
- Water Cannon: When attached to a top face, center is 0.5 above that face (per the official example). Length along its axis is 1.75; the ends are ±0.875 from its center along the axis.

STEP 1 — Structural placement and collision checks (block-by-block)

Base
- Base (given): [0,0,0].

  • Adjacent wood checks: to 63 Δx=0.75 → OK; to ledge 77 [−3,−5,9] z-sep=1 → OK.

Torches (blocks 81–84), all oriented purely outward (±x)
Torch body centers are 0.5 outboard from the mounting face; flame sphere centers are a further 1.0 along the outward direction; r=0.3.
- 81 on east face of 32 ([2,5,6]): body [3.0,5,6]; sphere center [4.0,5,6].
  • Clearance to leg east face plane x=2.5: 4.0−0.3−2.5=1.2 → OK.
  • Clearance to Wheel 79 outer face x=3.0: 4.0−0.3−3.0=0.7 → OK.
- 82 on west face of 42 ([−2,5,6]): body [−3.0,5,6]; sphere [−4.0,5,6].
  • Clearance to leg west face plane x=−2.5: (−2.5)−(−4.0+0.3)=1.2 → OK.
- 83 on east face of 52 ([2,−5,6]): body [3.0,−5,6]; sphere [4.0,−5,6].
  • Clearance to leg east face plane x=2.5: 1.2 → OK.
- 84 on west face of 62 ([−2,−5,6]): body [−3.0,−5,6]; sphere [−4.0,−5,6].
  • Clearance to Wheel 80 outer face x=−3.0: (−3.0)−(−4.0+0.3)=0.7 → OK.
All four flame spheres are fully outside all wood and wheels with ≥0.7 m margin; well beyond the 0.3 radius requirement.

Water cannon (block 85)
- Mounted on top of 78 (top face plane z=6.5), axis +z. Center at [0,0,7.0] (0.5 above mount face).
- Axial z-span [6.125,7.875]; x-span [−0.5,0.5]; y-span [−0.5,0.5].
- Neighbor check: to 78 center [0,0,6] Δz=1 (attach) → OK; to nearby deck and towers: nearest SWBs ≥2 units away in x or y and ≥1.5 in z → no overlap. Far from torches/wheels.

Braces (connectors; blocks 86–91)
Note: Braces are volumeless and must connect between faces of existing blocks.
- 86: Between 34 west face (x=1.5,y=5,z=8) and 44 east face (x=−1.5,y=5,z=8) → valid faces exist.
- 87: Between 54 west face (x=1.5,y=−5,z=8) and 64 east face (x=−1.5,y=−5,z=8) → valid.
- 88: Between 37 west face (x=1.5,y=5,z=2) and 47 east face (x=−1.5,y=5,z=2) → valid.
- 89: Between 57 west face (x=1.5,y=−5,z=2) and 67 east face (x=−1.5,y=−5,z=2) → valid.
- 90: Between 10 east face (x=0.5,y=5,z=5) and 37 west face (x=1.5,y=5,z=2). Note: Requires Block 30 (occupying 10’s east face) to be removed first, as you correctly noted.
- 91: Between 16 west face (x=−0.5,y=−5,z=5) and 67 east face (x=−1.5,y=−5,z=2). Requires Block 60 (occupying 16’s west face) to be removed first.

Winches (connectors; blocks 92–99)
Winches are volumeless; each connects two faces of existing blocks.
- 92: From 34 (−y face at y=4.5) to 9 (top face at z=5.5) → valid.
- 93: From 44 (−y face) to 9 (top face) → valid.
- 94: From 34 (−y face) to 7 (top face) → valid.
- 95: From 44 (−y face) to 7 (top face) → valid.
- 96: From 54 (+y face at y=−4.5) to 15 (top face at z=5.5) → valid.
- 97: From 64 (+y face) to 15 (top face) → valid.
- 98: From 54 (+y face) to 13 (top face) → valid.
- 99: From 64 (+y face) to 13 (top face) → valid.

Result of structural checks
- All SWBs and attachments are face-adjacent with separation 1 along the attach axis; no overlaps were detected.
- Powered wheels clear all wood by z and x gaps; torches’ flame spheres are now well clear of wood and wheels.
- Water cannon center/extent are correctly set; no collisions.

Note on assembly sequence consistency
- Your brace notes correctly state that 90 must be installed after removing temp arm 30 (to free Block 10’s east face) and 91 after removing 60 (to free Block 16’s west face). The general “Scaffold removal after winches and braces” line conflicts with this prerequisite. To avoid ambiguity:
  • Recommended sequence: (a) Install winches 92–99; (b) Remove 30 and 60; (c) Install braces 90 and 91; (d) Remove 40 and 50.
This yields the intended final state and satisfies face-access constraints.

STEP 2 — Functional validation

- Suspension and span:
  • Deck spine spans y=−6..+6 at z≈5, meeting the >10-unit span requirement.
  • Towers at y=±5, legs at x=±2, tops at z≈8–9, feet at z≈0 satisfy the prescribed geometry.
  • Winches 92–99 anchor from tower-top inner faces to deck top faces at y=±4 and ±2 as required. Ropes are volumeless; geometry is symmetric and feasible.

- Moving/functional components:
  • Powered Wheels 79,80 mounted on vertical faces with axes normal to those faces; planes vertical. Controls unbound/inactive; if spun, their kinematics (axis alignment and face-parallel plane) are consistent with the specified constraints. No horizontal-face mounting issues.
  • Water Cannon 85 mounted upright, center at z=7.0 per spec; controls unbound; no recoil will be produced. No heating/steam interactions since torches are far away and the cannon remains idle.
  • Torches 81–84 oriented purely outward (±x). Flame-sphere centers and clearances confirm no heating of any wood, ropes, wheels, or the cannon.

- Mounting/alignment:
  • All functional items are mounted to appropriate faces and respect orientation constraints. No moving component depends on a blocked axis or misaligned mounting.

STEP 3 — Physical feasibility

- The structure is a single piece after scaffold removal, retained by braces 90–91 and the tower-deck connections provided by the winches (connectors). The internal tower braces 86–89 provide rigidity.
- No collisions or interpenetrations are present in the final configuration. Feet at z=0 will rest on external banks as intended (environment-dependent, outside this review scope).
- Block count after scaffold removal:
  • SWB: 74 (1–78 minus 30,40,50,60)
  • Braces: 6
  • Winches: 8
  • Powered Wheels: 2
  • Torches: 4
  • Water Cannon: 1
  Total: 95 (+ base), satisfying the <100 requirement.

Final verdict
- Structural integrity and collision-free placement: PASS (with the clarified torch orientations and cannon center; no overlaps).
- Functional implementation: PASS (winch suspension geometry correct; wheels and cannon properly mounted and inactive; torch heat zones safe).
- Physical feasibility: PASS.

Minor documentation fix recommended: Update the scaffold-removal sequence wording to match the prerequisite noted for braces 90 and 91 (remove 30 and 60 before installing those braces). This does not affect the final geometry and is easy to correct.

The revised blueprint fully meets the design requirements. TERMINATE

Per attachment rule, center is 0.5 above the mount face, so at z=7.0 (Block 78 top face is at z=6.5). Axial span z≈[6.125, 7.875]. Leave control unbound; do not fire (no recoil).

Structural braces (functional connectors; volumeless, between faces)
86 - Brace - North tower top cross-tie - between Block 34 west face and Block 44 east face - Function and motion behavior: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 linking the two north tower legs for portal stiffness.
87 - Brace - South tower top cross-tie - between Block 54 west face and Block 64 east face - Function and motion behavior: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 linking the two south tower legs.
88 - Brace - North tower low cross-tie - between Block 37 west face and Block 47 east face - Function and motion behavior: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizing the north tower base.
89 - Brace - South tower low cross-tie - between Block 57 west face and Block 67 east face - Function and motion behavior: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizing the south tower base.
90 - Brace - North diagonal deck-to-leg tie - between Block 10 east face and Block 37 west face - Function and motion behavior: Install after removing temp arm Block 30 (face conflict). Preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability after scaffold removal.
91 - Brace - South diagonal deck-to-leg tie - between Block 16 west face and Block 67 east face - Function and motion behavior: Install after removing temp arm Block 60 (face conflict). Preserves unity and lateral stability.

Suspension winches (functional connectors; volumeless rope hangers)
92 - Winch - North tower hanger 1 - from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-east tower top. Leave default length for gentle sag.
93 - Winch - North tower hanger 2 - from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-west tower top.
94 - Winch - North tower hanger 3 - from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-east tower top.
95 - Winch - North tower hanger 4 - from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-west tower top.
96 - Winch - South tower hanger 1 - from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-east tower top.
97 - Winch - South tower hanger 2 - from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-west tower top.
98 - Winch - South tower hanger 3 - from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-east tower top.
99 - Winch - South tower hanger 4 - from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-west tower top.

Scaffold removal (after winches 92–99 are installed and diagonal braces 90–91 are in place)
- Remove Block 30 (temp arm at y=+5, x=+1), Block 40 (temp arm at y=+5, x=−1), Block 50 (temp arm at y=−5, x=+1), and Block 60 (temp arm at y=−5, x=−1). This frees the deck under each tower while maintaining a single-piece assembly via ropes and diagonal braces.

Functional behavior and controls
- Powered Wheels (79, 80): Decorative only; leave controls unbound/inactive so they remain braked.
- Torches (81–84): All flames oriented purely outward (±x). Each heating sphere (radius 0.3) is fully outside all wood, ropes, and powered wheels by ≥0.7 m.
- Water Cannon (85): Decorative finial; center at z=7.0 per attachment rule; leave control unbound; do not fire.
- Winches (92–99): Leave at default length; no active control required.

Position adjustment notes
- Torch orientation update: Oriented lantern flames purely outward (±x) to eliminate any intersection with wheel volumes or wooden leg faces; sphere centers now at x≈±4.0 with ≥0.7 m clearance.
- Water cannon center correction: Confirmed cannon center is 0.5 above Block 78 top face (z=6.5), i.e., z=7.0, per the official example.
- Wheel mounting clarification: Assumed wheel center ≈0.25 m outboard from the mounting face (thickness 0.5), disc radius 1, ensuring clearance from adjacent ledges and torches.

Final block count (after scaffold removal)
- Small Wooden Blocks: 74 (Blocks 1–78, minus removed 30, 40, 50, 60)
- Braces: 6
- Winches: 8
- Powered Wheels: 2
- Torches: 4
- Water Cannon: 1
Total: 95 (plus the default base), meeting the <100 requirement.

79 - Powered Wheel - North tower “clock-face” on east leg, centered at z≈7 - east of Block 33 - Function and motion behavior: Decorative powered wheel on a vertical east face; wheel axis along +x (into the leg), wheel plane vertical. If activated, it would spin; leave control unbound/inactive so it remains braked. Mounting offset: center ≈0.25 m outboard from the leg face; thickness 0.5; disc radius 1.
80 - Powered Wheel - South tower “clock-face” on west leg, centered at z≈7 - west of Block 63 - Function and motion behavior: Decorative powered wheel on a vertical west face; axis along −x; plane vertical. Leave control unbound/inactive. Same mounting offset as above.

Torches as lanterns (functional blocks; oriented outward for fire safety)
81 - Torch - Lantern on north tower east leg at z≈6, flame purely outward (+x) - east of Block 32 - Function and motion behavior: Body ~0.5 m outboard from the leg face; flame sphere center ~1.0 m further outward at x≈+4.0, y=+5, z=6; radius 0.3. ≥0.7 m clear of any wood or Wheel 79.
82 - Torch - Lantern on north tower west leg at z≈6, flame purely outward (−x) - west of Block 42 - Function and motion behavior: Flame sphere center at x≈−4.0, y=+5, z=6; r=0.3. ≥0.7 m clear of the leg west face and far from ropes/wheel.
83 - Torch - Lantern on south tower east leg at z≈6, flame purely outward (+x) - east of Block 52 - Function and motion behavior: Flame sphere center at x≈+4.0, y=−5, z=6; r=0.3. ≥0.7 m clear of the leg east face and ropes.
84 - Torch - Lantern on south tower west leg at z≈6, flame purely outward (−x) - west of Block 62 - Function and motion behavior: Flame sphere center at x≈−4.0, y=−5, z=6; r=0.3. ≥0.7 m clear of Wheel 80 and wood.

Water cannon finial (functional block; left idle)
85 - Water Cannon - Decorative finial centered above deck (y≈0), pointing up - top of Block 78 - Function and motion behavior: Axis vertical (+z). Per attachment rule, center is 0.5 above the mount face, so at z=7.0 (Block 78 top face is at z=6.5). Axial span z≈[6.125, 7.875]. Leave control unbound; do not fire (no recoil).

Structural braces (functional connectors; volumeless, between faces)
86 - Brace - North tower top cross-tie - between Block 34 west face and Block 44 east face - Function and motion behavior: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 linking the two north tower legs for portal stiffness.
87 - Brace - South tower top cross-tie - between Block 54 west face and Block 64 east face - Function and motion behavior: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 linking the two south tower legs.
88 - Brace - North tower low cross-tie - between Block 37 west face and Block 47 east face - Function and motion behavior: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizing the north tower base.
89 - Brace - South tower low cross-tie - between Block 57 west face and Block 67 east face - Function and motion behavior: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizing the south tower base.
90 - Brace - North diagonal deck-to-leg tie - between Block 10 east face and Block 37 west face - Function and motion behavior: Install after removing temp arm Block 30 (face conflict). Preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability after scaffold removal.
91 - Brace - South diagonal deck-to-leg tie - between Block 16 west face and Block 67 east face - Function and motion behavior: Install after removing temp arm Block 60 (face conflict). Preserves unity and lateral stability.

Suspension winches (functional connectors; volumeless rope hangers)
92 - Winch - North tower hanger 1 - from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-east tower top. Leave default length for gentle sag.
93 - Winch - North tower hanger 2 - from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-west tower top.
94 - Winch - North tower hanger 3 - from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-east tower top.
95 - Winch - North tower hanger 4 - from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-west tower top.
96 - Winch - South tower hanger 1 - from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-east tower top.
97 - Winch - South tower hanger 2 - from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-west tower top.
98 - Winch - South tower hanger 3 - from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-east tower top.
99 - Winch - South tower hanger 4 - from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Function and motion behavior: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-west tower top.

Scaffold removal (after winches 92–99 are installed and diagonal braces 90–91 are in place)
- Remove Block 30 (temp arm at y=+5, x=+1), Block 40 (temp arm at y=+5, x=−1), Block 50 (temp arm at y=−5, x=+1), and Block 60 (temp arm at y=−5, x=−1). This frees the deck under each tower while maintaining a single-piece assembly via ropes and diagonal braces.

Functional behavior and controls
- Powered Wheels (79, 80): Decorative only; leave controls unbound/inactive so they remain braked.
- Torches (81–84): All flames oriented purely outward (±x). Each heating sphere (radius 0.3) is fully outside all wood, ropes, and powered wheels by ≥0.7 m.
- Water Cannon (85): Decorative finial; center at z=7.0 per attachment rule; leave control unbound; do not fire.
- Winches (92–99): Leave at default length; no active control required.

Position adjustment notes
- Torch orientation update: Oriented lantern flames purely outward (±x) to eliminate any intersection with wheel volumes or wooden leg faces; sphere centers now at x≈±4.0 with ≥0.7 m clearance.
- Water cannon center correction: Confirmed cannon center is 0.5 above Block 78 top face (z=6.5), i.e., z=7.0, per the official example.
- Wheel mounting clarification: Assumed wheel center ≈0.25 m outboard from the mounting face (thickness 0.5), disc radius 1, ensuring clearance from adjacent ledges and torches.

Final block count (after scaffold removal)
- Small Wooden Blocks: 74 (Blocks 1–78, minus removed 30, 40, 50, 60)
- Braces: 6
- Winches: 8
- Powered Wheels: 2
- Torches: 4
- Water Cannon: 1
Total: 95 (plus the default base), meeting the <100 requirement.
<end blueprint>

title: builder thought
summary: Introduction to available build tools and how we’ll use them step by step I will construct your structure one block at a time, following your blueprint exactly. Here are the tools I can use and what I need from you (or…
body:
Introduction to available build tools and how we’ll use them step by step

I will construct your structure one block at a time, following your blueprint exactly. Here are the tools I can use and what I need from you (or from the current build state) to use them effectively:

- functions.start
  - What it does: Creates the very first block (the starting base) and lets us choose its initial position and rotation.
  - Inputs needed: init_shift [x, y, z], init_rotation [yaw, pitch, roll] in degrees, and a descriptive note.
  - When we’ll use it: Right now, to establish the default 1×1×1 base at [0,0,0].
  - Note: The note field is very helpful to label the overall machine purpose.

- functions.attach_block_to
  - What it does: Attaches a new block to a specific face of an existing block. Available new_block types: Powered Wheel, Small Wooden Block, Torch, Water Cannon.
  - Inputs needed: base_block ID, face (e.g., Top, Bottom, North, South, East, West), new_block type, and an optional note describing the function (highly recommended).
  - Why the note is important: The note argument is a powerful identifier to mark functional intent (e.g., “North tower east leg z=7”), which is invaluable for later references, inspections, and removal tasks.

- functions.connect_blocks
  - What it does: Connects two existing blocks using a connector (Brace or Winch) between specified faces. Will fail if faces are too close or invalid.
  - Inputs needed: block_a ID and face_a, block_b ID and face_b, connector type, and an optional note (highly recommended).
  - Why the note is important: Precisely records the purpose (e.g., “North tower hanger 1”), making audits and adjustments straightforward.

- functions.remove_block
  - What it does: Removes a block (helpful for temporary scaffolds you’ve planned to remove).
  - Inputs needed: block_id.

- functions.shift_block
  - What it does: Shifts an already-placed block by a small offset in 3D space. Range for each axis is [-0.5, 0.5]. Exceeding this risks detachment.
  - Inputs needed: block_id and shift_real [dx, dy, dz].
  - When useful: Fine adjustments after attachment, e.g., positioning decorative powered wheels outward by ~0.25 m, or ensuring torch clearances.

- functions.twist_block
  - What it does: Rotates a block around its rooted surface by a given angle in degrees. Useful for orientation of pointer-like or directional parts after attachment.
  - Inputs needed: block_id and angle.

- functions.flip_spin
  - What it does: Flips spin direction for spinning blocks (only if the block has a spin property, e.g., Powered Wheel).
  - Inputs needed: block_id.

- functions.get_block_details
  - What it does: Returns complete details (position, rotation, labeled faces) for a specific block.
  - Inputs needed: block_id.
  - When useful: To confirm valid face names before attaching or connecting.

- functions.get_machine_summary
  - What it does: Provides the latest overview of the machine. Mandatory to run for a final check before we end the build session; I will always remind you to do this as a final validation step.

Build approach and notes for your blueprint
- I’ll place blocks exactly as specified, one block and one tool call per step.
- I’ll leverage the note field on each attach/connect operation to label each block and connector precisely as per your numbering and description (very important for clarity and later removal of temp scaffolds).
- For items with offsets/orientation (Powered Wheels, Torches, Water Cannon), I’ll attach to the correct face first, then twist/shift if needed to satisfy outward orientation, mounting offset, and clearance requirements per your position adjustment notes.
- After installing winches and diagonal braces, I’ll remove the specified temporary scaffold arms.
- At the end, we’ll run get_machine_summary for a mandatory final audit.

Proceeding with Step 0: Initialize the build with the default base at [0,0,0]. Then I will attach Block 1 (kingpost z=1) to the top of the base.

Executing: Create the starting base now.
